name: snowplow-tracker

metadata.name: snowplow-tracker
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Snowplow event tracker for Python. Add analytics to your Python and Django apps, webapps and games

documentation:
Python Analytics for Snowplow
=============================

[![Early Release](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?style=flat&label=Snowplow&message=Early%20Release&color=014477&labelColor=9ba0aa&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://docs.snowplow.io/docs/collecting-data/collecting-from-own-applications/tracker-maintenance-classification/)[![Build Status](https://github.com/snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker/actions)[![Test Coverage](https://img.shields.io/coveralls/github/snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker)](https://coveralls.io/github/snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker?branch=master) [![image](http://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2-blue.svg?style=flat)](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)


[![Pypi Snowplow Tracker](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/snowplow-tracker)](https://pypi.org/project/snowplow-tracker/)[![Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/snowplow-tracker)](https://pypi.org/project/snowplow-tracker/)[![Monthly Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/snowplow-tracker)](https://pypi.org/project/snowplow-tracker/)

Overview
--------

Add analytics to your Python apps and Python games with the
[Snowplow](http://snowplow.io) event tracker for
[Python](http://python.org).

With this tracker you can collect event data from your Python-based
applications, games or Python web servers/frameworks.

Find out more
-------------

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  | Snowplow Docs | API Docs  | Contributing |
  |     :----:     |     :----:   |     :----:   |
  | ![techdocs](https://d3i6fms1cm1j0i.cloudfront.net/github/images/techdocs.png) | ![setup](https://d3i6fms1cm1j0i.cloudfront.net/github/images/setup.png) |                                                ![contributing](https://d3i6fms1cm1j0i.cloudfront.net/github/images/contributing.png) |
  | [Snowplow Docs](https://docs.snowplow.io/docs/collecting-data/collecting-from-own-applications/python-tracker/) | [API Docs](https://snowplow.github.io/snowplow-python-tracker/index.html)| [Contributing](https://github.com/snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) |                                                                              
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Python Support
--------------

| Python version | snowplow-tracker version |
|     :----:     |     :----:               |
| \>=3.5         | > 0.10.0                 |
| 2.7            | > 0.9.1                  |

Maintainer Quickstart
---------------------

Assuming [docker](https://www.docker.com/) is installed

    host$ git clone git@github.com:snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker.git
    host$ cd snowplow-python-tracker
    host$ docker build -t snowplow-python-tracker . && docker run snowplow-python-tracker

Copyright and license
---------------------

Ltd.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.__init__
documentation:
Configuration for the emitter that sends events to the Snowplow collector.
\:param batch_size:     The maximum number of queued events before the buffer is flushed. Default is 10.
\:type  batch_size:     int | None
\:param on_success:      Callback executed after every HTTP request in a flush has status code 200

> Gets passed one argument, an array of dictionaries corresponding to the sent events' payloads

```
type  on_success

function | None

param on_failure

Callback executed if at least one HTTP request in a flush has status code other than 200
Gets passed two arguments:
1) The number of events which were successfully sent
2) An array of dictionaries corresponding to the unsent events' payloads

type  on_failure

function | None

param byte_limit

The size event list after reaching which queued events will be flushed

type  byte_limit

int | None

param request_timeout

Timeout for the HTTP requests. Can be set either as single float value which
applies to both "connect" AND "read" timeout, or as tuple with two float values
which specify the "connect" and "read" timeouts separately

type request_timeout

float | tuple | None

param  custom_retry_codes

Set custom retry rules for HTTP status codes received in emit responses from the Collector.
By default, retry will not occur for status codes 400, 401, 403, 410 or 422. This can be overridden here.
Note that 2xx codes will never retry as they are considered successful.

type   custom_retry_codes

dict

param  event_store

Stores the event buffer and buffer capacity. Default is an InMemoryEventStore object with buffer_capacity of 10,000 events.

type   event_store

EventStore | None

param  session

Persist parameters across requests by using a session object

type   session

request.Session | None
```

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.batch_size
documentation: The maximum number of queued events before the buffer is flushed. Default is 10.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.on_success
documentation: Callback executed after every HTTP request in a flush has status code 200. Gets passed the number of events flushed.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.on_failure
documentation:
Callback executed if at least one HTTP request in a flush has status code other than 200
                        Gets passed two arguments:
                        1) The number of events which were successfully sent
                        2) An array of dictionaries corresponding to the unsent events' payloads

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.byte_limit
documentation: The size event list after reaching which queued events will be flushed

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.request_timeout
documentation:
Timeout for the HTTP requests. Can be set either as single float value which
                             applies to both "connect" AND "read" timeout, or as tuple with two float values
                             which specify the "connect" and "read" timeouts separately

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.buffer_capacity
documentation:
The maximum capacity of the event buffer. The default buffer capacity is 10 000 events.
                        When the buffer is full new events are lost.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.custom_retry_codes
documentation: Custom retry rules for HTTP status codes received in emit responses from the Collector.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.set_retry_code
documentation:
Add a retry rule for HTTP status code received from emit responses from the Collector.
:param  status_code:    HTTP response code
:type   status_code:    int
:param  retry:  Set the status_code to retry (True) or not retry (False). Default is True
:type   retry:  bool

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitter_configuration.EmitterConfiguration.session
documentation: Persist parameters across requests using a requests.Session object

classes.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Requester
documentation: Unifes the two request methods under one interface

classes.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.__init__
documentation:
---
param  custom_retry_codes: |-
  Set custom retry rules for HTTP status codes received in emit responses from the Collector.
  By default, retry will not occur for status codes 400, 401, 403, 410 or 422. This can be overridden here.
  Note that 2xx codes will never retry as they are considered successful.
param  event_store: Stores the event buffer and buffer capacity. Default is an InMemoryEventStore
  object with buffer_capacity of 10,000 events.
param  session: Persist parameters across requests by using a session object
param batch_size: The maximum number of queued events before the buffer is flushed.
  Default is 10.
param buffer_capacity: |-
  The maximum capacity of the event buffer.
  When the buffer is full new events are lost.
param byte_limit: The size event list after reaching which queued events will be flushed
param endpoint: The collector URL. If protocol is not set in endpoint it will automatically
  set to "<https://>" - this is done automatically.
param max_retry_delay_seconds: Set the maximum time between attempts to send failed
  events to the collector. Default 60 seconds
param method: The HTTP request method. Defaults to post.
param on_failure: |-
  Callback executed if at least one HTTP request in a flush has status code other than 200
  Gets passed two arguments:
  1\) The number of events which were successfully sent
  2\) An array of dictionaries corresponding to the unsent events' payloads
param on_success: |-
  Callback executed after every HTTP request in a flush has status code 200
  Gets passed one argument, an array of dictionaries corresponding to the sent events' payloads
param port: The collector port to connect to
param protocol: The protocol to use - http or https. Defaults to https.
param request_timeout: |-
  Timeout for the HTTP requests. Can be set either as single float value which
  applies to both "connect" AND "read" timeout, or as tuple with two float values
  which specify the "connect" and "read" timeouts separately
type   custom_retry_codes: dict
type   event_store: EventStore | None
type   session: requests.Session | None
type  batch_size: int | None
type  byte_limit: int | None
type  endpoint: string
type  method: method
type  on_failure: function | None
type  on_success: function | None
type  port: int | None
type  protocol: protocol
type buffer_capacity: int
type max_retry_delay_seconds: int
type request_timeout: float | tuple | None
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.as_collector_uri
documentation:
---
param endpoint: The raw endpoint provided by the user
param method: Either `get` or `post` HTTP method
param port: The collector port to connect to
param protocol: The protocol to use - http or https
rtype: string
type  endpoint: string
type  method: method
type  port: int | None
type  protocol: protocol
---

Synchronously send Snowplow events to a Snowplow collector
Supports both GET and POST requests

classes.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.AsyncEmitter

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.AsyncEmitter.__init__
documentation:
---
param  event_store: Stores the event buffer and buffer capacity. Default is an InMemoryEventStore
  object with buffer_capacity of 10,000 events.
param  session: Persist parameters across requests by using a session object
param batch_size: The maximum number of queued events before the buffer is flushed.
  Default is 10.
param buffer_capacity: |-
  The maximum capacity of the event buffer.
  When the buffer is full new events are lost.
param byte_limit: The size event list after reaching which queued events will be flushed
param endpoint: The collector URL. If protocol is not set in endpoint it will automatically
  set to "<https://>" - this is done automatically.
param max_retry_delay_seconds: Set the maximum time between attempts to send failed
  events to the collector. Default 60 seconds
param method: The HTTP request method
param on_failure: |-
  Callback executed if at least one HTTP request in a flush has status code other than 200
  Gets passed two arguments:
  1\) The number of events which were successfully sent
  2\) An array of dictionaries corresponding to the unsent events' payloads
param on_success: |-
  Callback executed after every HTTP request in a flush has status code 200
  Gets passed one argument, an array of dictionaries corresponding to the sent events' payloads
param port: The collector port to connect to
param protocol: The protocol to use - http or https. Defaults to http.
param thread_count: Number of worker threads to use for HTTP requests
type   event_store: EventStore
type   session: requests.Session | None
type  batch_size: int | None
type  byte_limit: int | None
type  endpoint: string
type  method: method
type  on_failure: function | None
type  on_success: function | None
type  port: int | None
type  protocol: protocol
type  thread_count: int
type buffer_capacity: int
type max_retry_delay_seconds: int
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.AsyncEmitter.flush
documentation:
Removes all dead threads, then creates a new thread which
executes the flush method of the base Emitter class
Uses threads to send HTTP requests asynchronously

classes.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.FlushTimer
documentation: Internal class used by the Emitter to schedule flush calls for later.

Constructs a SelfDescribing event object.

This is a customisable event type which allows you to track anything describable
by a JsonSchema.

When tracked, generates a self-describing event (event type "ue").

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.events.structured_event.StructuredEvent.value
documentation: A value associated with the user action

Constructs a Structured event object.

This event type is provided to be roughly equivalent to Google Analytics-style events.
Note that it is not automatically clear what data should be placed in what field.
To aid data quality and modeling, agree on business-wide definitions when designing
your tracking strategy.

We recommend using SelfDescribing - fully custom - events instead.

When tracked, generates a "struct" or "se" event.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.events.event.Event.true_timestamp
documentation: Optional event timestamp in milliseconds

Event class which contains
elements that can be set in all events. These are context, trueTimestamp, and Subject.

Context is a list of custom SelfDescribingJson entities.
TrueTimestamp is a user-defined timestamp.
Subject is an event-specific Subject. Its fields will override those of the
Tracker-associated Subject, if present.

:type   name:           string_or_none
:param  category:       Item's category
:type   category:       string_or_none
:param  unit_price:     Item's price
:type   unit_price:     int | float | None
:param  currency:       Type of currency the price is in
:type   currency:       string_or_none
:param  context:        Custom context for the event
:type   context:        context_array | None
:param  tstamp:         Optional event timestamp in milliseconds
:type   tstamp:         int | float | None
:param  event_subject:  Optional per event subject
:type   event_subject:  subject | None
:rtype:                 Tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.track_ecommerce_transaction_item
documentation:
This is an internal method called by track_ecommerce_transaction.
It is not for public use.

:param  order_id:       Order ID
:type   order_id:       non_empty_string
:param  sku:            Item SKU

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.track_mobile_screen_view
documentation:
:param  name:           The name of the screen view event
:type   name:           string_or_none
:param  id_:            Screen view ID. This must be of type UUID.
:type   id_:            string | None
:param  type:           The type of screen that was viewed e.g feed / carousel.
:type   type:           string | None
:param  previous_name:  The name of the previous screen.
:type   previous_name:  string | None
:param  previous_id:    The screenview ID of the previous screenview.
:type   previous_id:    string | None
:param  previous_type   The screen type of the previous screenview
:type   previous_type   string | None
:param  transition_type The type of transition that led to the screen being viewed.
:type   transition_type string | None
:param  context:        Custom context for the event
:type   context:        context_array | None
:param  tstamp:         Optional event timestamp in milliseconds
:type   tstamp:         int | float | None
:param  event_subject:  Optional per event subject
:type   event_subject:  subject | None

documentation:
:param  category:       Category of the event
:type   category:       non_empty_string
:param  action:         The event itself
:type   action:         non_empty_string
:param  label:          Refer to the object the action is
                        performed on
:type   label:          string_or_none
:param  property_:      Property associated with either the action
                        or the object
:type   property_:      string_or_none
:param  value:          A value associated with the user action
:type   value:          int | float | None

:param  tstamp:         Optional event timestamp in milliseconds
:type   tstamp:         int | float | None
:param  event_subject:  Optional per event subject
:type   event_subject:  subject | None
:rtype:                 Tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.track_self_describing_event
documentation:
:param  event_json:      The properties of the event. Has two field:
                         A "data" field containing the event properties and
                         A "schema" field identifying the schema against which the data is validated
:type   event_json:      self_describing_json
:param  context:         Custom context for the event
:type   context:         context_array | None
:param  tstamp:          Optional event timestamp in milliseconds
:type   tstamp:          int | float | None
:param  event_subject:   Optional per event subject
:type   event_subject:   subject | None
:rtype:                  Tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.flush
documentation:
Flush the emitter

:type   is_async:  bool
:rtype:         tracker

subject | None
